code_changes.content:
# Kubernetes RBAC Authorization Flow — Security Analysis

## Files Examined

| File | Role in RBAC Authorization Flow |
|------|----------------------------------|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go` | Assembles the HTTP handler chain; defines `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` which sets filter order |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/requestinfo.go` | `WithRequestInfo` — parses HTTP URL into structured `RequestInfo` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request/requestinfo.go` | `RequestInfoFactory.NewRequestInfo` — extracts verb, resource, namespace, name, selectors from URL |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go` | `WithAuthentication` — authenticates request, sets `user.Info` on context |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation.go` | `WithImpersonation` — authorizes and processes impersonation headers before main authz |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/audit.go` | `WithAudit` — audit event lifecycle around request handling |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go` | `WithAuthorization` — authorization filter; assembles `AttributesRecord`, calls authorizer chain |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go` | Core interfaces: `Authorizer`, `Attributes`, `AttributesRecord`, `Decision` enum |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer/rule.go` | `ResourceRuleInfo` / `NonResourceRuleInfo` interface definitions |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/union/union.go` | `unionAuthzHandler` — chains multiple authorizers; short-circuit on Allow/Deny |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizerfactory/builtin.go` | `alwaysAllowAuthorizer`, `alwaysDenyAuthorizer`, `privilegedGroupAuthorizer` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizerfactory/delegating.go` | `DelegatingAuthorizerConfig` — constructs webhook authorizer for delegated auth |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/path/path.go` | Path-based allow-list authorizer for non-resource endpoints |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/metrics/metrics.go` | `instrumentedAuthorizer` — wraps each authorizer to emit decision metrics |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user/user.go` | `user.Info` interface and `DefaultInfo` struct; well-known group constants |
| `pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/config.go` | `Config.New` — constructs RBAC/Node/ABAC/Webhook authorizers from configuration |
| `pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/reload.go` | `reloadableAuthorizerResolver` — hot-reloads authorizer config; `newForConfig` assembles chain |
| `plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go` | `RBACAuthorizer.Authorize`, `RuleAllows`, `authorizingVisitor` |
| `plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go` | `SubjectAccessEvaluator.AllowedSubjects` — reverse-lookup of subjects for an action |
| `plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/policy.go` | Default cluster roles and bindings installed at cluster bootstrap |
| `pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go` | `DefaultRuleResolver` — resolves roles/bindings to `PolicyRule` lists; `ConfirmNoEscalation` |

---

## Entry Points

### 1. API Request Entry Points

**Primary HTTP entry point:** Every inbound API request flows through the handler chain built by `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:1004`. The chain is constructed in reverse; at runtime filters execute in this order:

1. `WithPanicRecovery` / `WithAuditInit`
2. `WithRequestInfo` — parses the URL path into structured fields
3. `WithAuthentication` — establishes identity
4. `WithAudit` — begins audit event
5. `WithImpersonation` — may substitute identity
6. Priority/Fairness or MaxInFlight throttling
7. `WithAuthorization` — makes the final Allow/Deny decision
8. API handler (the authorized business logic)

**Untrusted inputs entering the URL-parsing stage** (`request/requestinfo.go:RequestInfoFactory.NewRequestInfo`):
- HTTP method, URL path, query parameters (`fieldSelector`, `labelSelector`, `watch`)
- These directly populate `RequestInfo.{Verb, Resource, Namespace, Name, Subresource, FieldSelector, LabelSelector}`

**Untrusted inputs entering the authentication stage** (`filters/authentication.go`):
- `Authorization` HTTP header (Bearer tokens, Basic auth)
- Client TLS certificates (x509 authenticator)
- `X-Remote-User`, `X-Remote-Group`, `X-Remote-Extra-*` headers (proxy authenticator — see mitigations below)

**Untrusted inputs entering the impersonation stage** (`filters/impersonation.go`):
- `Impersonate-User`, `Impersonate-Group`, `Impersonate-Uid`, `Impersonate-Extra-*` HTTP headers
- Each impersonation request is separately authorized before the identity is substituted

### 2. RBAC Policy Data Entry Points

RBAC policy objects (Roles, ClusterRoles, RoleBindings, ClusterRoleBindings) enter the authorization path through the **shared informer cache**:

- `rbac.RoleGetter` wraps `rbaclisters.RoleLister` (in-memory informer cache)
- `rbac.RoleBindingLister` wraps `rbaclisters.RoleBindingLister`
- `rbac.ClusterRoleGetter` wraps `rbaclisters.ClusterRoleLister`
- `rbac.ClusterRoleBindingLister` wraps `rbaclisters.ClusterRoleBindingLister`

These are passed into `rbac.New()` at `pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/config.go:123`. The authorizer **never reads etcd directly** during authorization; it reads the in-memory reflector cache. Policy changes reach the cache asynchronously via the watch stream.

**Authorization configuration file** (`pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/config.go:163` — `LoadAndValidateFile`): The authorizer chain itself can be hot-reloaded from a YAML file on disk, read and parsed every minute by `runReload` (`reload.go:185`).

### 3. Untrusted Input in Authorization Decisions

| Input | Path | Risk |
|-------|------|------|
| URL path & verb | `NewRequestInfo` → `AttributesRecord` | Determines what resource/action is being authorized |
| User identity (name, groups) | Authenticator → `user.Info` on context | Determines which bindings apply |
| `X-Remote-User` / `X-Remote-Group` headers | Proxy authenticator → `user.Info` | Spoofable if front-proxy cert validation is misconfigured |
| Impersonation headers | `buildImpersonationRequests` → authorized substitution | Can change effective identity if impersonation RBAC is too permissive |
| Label/field selectors | `requestinfo.go` → `AttributesRecord` (feature-gated) | Used in authorization with `AuthorizeWithSelectors` feature gate |
| Authorization config YAML file | `checkFile` / `LoadAndValidateData` | Config hot-reload path; file on disk must be protected |

---

## Data Flow

### Stage 1 — Request Arrival

**File:** `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/requestinfo.go`
**Function:** `WithRequestInfo(handler http.Handler, resolver request.RequestInfoResolver) http.Handler`

The raw `http.Request` is parsed by `RequestInfoFactory.NewRequestInfo`. The result is a `request.RequestInfo` struct stored on the request context via `request.WithRequestInfo(ctx, info)`.

**Data structure: `request.RequestInfo`** (`request/requestinfo.go:44`):
```
IsResourceRequest bool
Path, Verb, APIPrefix, APIGroup, APIVersion string
Namespace, Resource, Subresource, Name string
Parts []string
FieldSelector, LabelSelector string
```

The verb is translated from HTTP method to Kubernetes verb (e.g., GET → get/list/watch, POST → create). The namespace, resource type, and object name are extracted from the URL path structure (e.g., `/api/v1/namespaces/default/pods/mypod`).

### Stage 2 — Authentication

**File:** `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go`
**Function:** `withAuthentication(handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, metrics)`

The union `authenticator.Request` is invoked: `resp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)`.

On success:
- Audience validation is performed (`audiencesAreAcceptable`)
- The `Authorization` header is deleted from the request (`req.Header.Del("Authorization")`)
- Standard and custom proxy headers (`X-Remote-User`, `X-Remote-Group`, `X-Remote-Extra-*`, `X-Remote-Uid`) are **cleared** via `headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders`
- The authenticated `user.Info` is stored on context: `genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User)`
- Anonymous HTTP/2 connections receive `Connection: close` (CVE-2023-44487 mitigation)

**Data structure: `user.Info`** (`authentication/user/user.go:20`):
```
GetName() string       // e.g., "system:serviceaccount:default:mysa"
GetUID() string
GetGroups() []string   // e.g., ["system:authenticated", "devs"]
GetExtra() map[string][]string
```

On failure: the `failedHandler` returns HTTP 401. Failed authentication is also emitted to the audit backend via `WithFailedAuthenticationAudit`.

### Stage 3 — Impersonation Filter

**File:** `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation.go`
**Function:** `WithImpersonation(handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer)`

This filter runs **before** `WithAuthorization`. It reads impersonation headers and, for each target identity component (User, Group, UID, UserExtra), constructs an `authorizer.AttributesRecord` with verb `"impersonate"` and calls `a.Authorize(ctx, actingAsAttributes)`. Only if every component is authorized does the filter replace the user on the context with the new `user.DefaultInfo`.

After substitution, all impersonation headers are deleted from the request. The impersonation event is logged to the audit record via `audit.LogImpersonatedUser`.

### Stage 4 — Authorization Filter

**File:** `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go`
**Function:** `WithAuthorization(handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer)`

**Step 4a — Attribute assembly** via `GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)`:
- Reads `user.Info` from context (set by authentication/impersonation stages)
- Reads `RequestInfo` from context (set by `WithRequestInfo`)
- Optionally parses field/label selectors (when `AuthorizeWithSelectors` feature gate is enabled)
- Constructs `authorizer.AttributesRecord`

**Data structure: `authorizer.AttributesRecord`** (`authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go:104`):
```
User            user.Info
Verb            string  // get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, proxy, ...
Namespace       string
APIGroup        string
APIVersion      string
Resource        string
Subresource     string
Name            string
ResourceRequest bool
Path            string
FieldSelectorRequirements fields.Requirements
LabelSelectorRequirements labels.Requirements
```

**Step 4b — Authorization decision**: `authorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)`

The result is of type `authorizer.Decision` (an int):
- `DecisionAllow` (1): request proceeds, audit annotation `authorization.k8s.io/decision: allow`
- `DecisionDeny` (2): HTTP 403, audit annotation `authorization.k8s.io/decision: forbid`
- `DecisionNoOpinion` (0): treated as deny by the filter (HTTP 403)

### Stage 4 (inner) — RBAC Evaluation

**File:** `plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go`
**Function:** `RBACAuthorizer.Authorize(ctx, requestAttributes authorizer.Attributes)`

The RBAC authorizer uses a visitor pattern. An `authorizingVisitor` is passed to `VisitRulesFor`:

```go
r.authorizationRuleResolver.VisitRulesFor(ctx, requestAttributes.GetUser(),
    requestAttributes.GetNamespace(), ruleCheckingVisitor.visit)
```

**`DefaultRuleResolver.VisitRulesFor`** (`pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go:179`):

1. Lists all `ClusterRoleBinding` objects from the informer cache
2. For each binding, calls `appliesTo(user, clusterRoleBinding.Subjects, "")`:
   - `UserKind`: exact string match on `user.GetName()`
   - `GroupKind`: membership check in `user.GetGroups()`
   - `ServiceAccountKind`: `serviceaccount.MatchesUsername(namespace, name, user.GetName())`
3. If the user matches, calls `GetRoleReferenceRules(ctx, binding.RoleRef, "")` to resolve the referenced `ClusterRole` or `Role` to its `[]rbacv1.PolicyRule`
4. Passes each `PolicyRule` to the visitor
5. Repeats for namespace-scoped `RoleBinding` objects (if `namespace != ""`)

**`RuleAllows`** (`rbac.go:178`) performs the policy match:
- Resource requests: checks verb, API group, resource (including subresource), and resource name against wildcard-aware matchers (`VerbMatches`, `APIGroupMatches`, `ResourceMatches`, `ResourceNameMatches`)
- Non-resource requests: checks verb and URL path against wildcard matchers

**RBAC returns `DecisionNoOpinion` on denial, never `DecisionDeny`.** This is by design: RBAC only allows; it defers to other authorizers or to the final implicit deny.

### Stage 5 — Decision and Audit

**Authorization filter** (`authorization.go:79`):
- `DecisionAllow`: adds audit annotations, calls the next handler
- Error without allow: returns HTTP 500
- Any other outcome: logs the denial, adds audit annotations (`decision: forbid`), calls `responsewriters.Forbidden` (HTTP 403)

**Audit subsystem** (`filters/audit.go:42`, `WithAudit`):
- Wraps the response writer to capture the HTTP status code
- Emits `StageRequestReceived` event before the handler runs
- Emits `StageResponseComplete` (or `StageResponseStarted` for streaming) after completion
- The authorization decision is recorded as structured annotations on the audit event via `audit.AddAuditAnnotations`

---

## Dependency Chain

Ordered from request entry to authorization decision:

```
1.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go
    DefaultBuildHandlerChain — assembles all filters

2.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/requestinfo.go
    WithRequestInfo → resolver.NewRequestInfo(req)

3.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request/requestinfo.go
    RequestInfoFactory.NewRequestInfo — parses URL into RequestInfo

4.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go
    WithAuthentication → auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)
    → stores user.Info on context; clears auth headers

5.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/audit.go
    WithAudit — starts audit event

6.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation.go
    WithImpersonation → buildImpersonationRequests(req.Header)
    → a.Authorize(ctx, actingAsAttributes) [for each impersonation component]
    → replaces user on context if authorized

7.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go
    WithAuthorization → GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)
    → a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)

8.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/union/union.go
    unionAuthzHandler.Authorize — iterates authorizer chain

9.  staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizerfactory/builtin.go
    privilegedGroupAuthorizer.Authorize — checked first; allows system:masters

10. pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/reload.go
    reloadableAuthorizerResolver → newForConfig → union.New(...)
    (wraps each sub-authorizer with authorizationmetrics.InstrumentedAuthorizer)

11. plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go
    RBACAuthorizer.Authorize → authorizingVisitor.visit

12. pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go
    DefaultRuleResolver.VisitRulesFor
    → clusterRoleBindingLister.ListClusterRoleBindings (informer cache)
    → appliesTo(user, subjects, namespace)
    → GetRoleReferenceRules(ctx, roleRef, namespace)
    → roleGetter.GetRole / clusterRoleGetter.GetClusterRole (informer cache)

13. plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go
    RuleAllows → rbacv1helpers.VerbMatches / APIGroupMatches / ResourceMatches / ResourceNameMatches

14. staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go
    Decision → audit annotations → allow or HTTP 403
```

---

## Analysis

### Vulnerability Classes

#### 1. Authentication Bypass (Low Risk — Strongly Mitigated)

**X-Remote-User/Group header injection** is a historical Kubernetes attack surface. An attacker who can send requests directly to the API server with spoofed `X-Remote-User` headers could bypass authentication — but only if the request comes from an IP that the API server trusts as a proxy, or if the front-proxy certificate validation is disabled/misconfigured.

Mitigation in code: `authentication.go:91–107` calls `headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders` for both standard (`X-Remote-User`, `X-Remote-Group`, `X-Remote-Extra-*`, `X-Remote-Uid`) and any custom proxy headers **after** authentication succeeds with the _real_ authenticator. Proxy authentication itself requires a mutually-authenticated TLS client certificate matching the configured proxy CA. If the request was not authenticated via the proxy authenticator, the headers are still cleared before passing to the next handler, preventing downstream code from reading them.

#### 2. Privilege Escalation via Impersonation

**Attack scenario:** A user or service account with the `impersonate` verb on `users`, `groups`, or `serviceaccounts` resources can present any identity to the API server, including `system:masters` group membership.

**Code path:** `impersonation.go:117` calls `a.Authorize(ctx, actingAsAttributes)` for each impersonation component. The `actingAsAttributes` uses the **pre-impersonation** real identity of the requestor. If authorized, the new identity (including group membership) is committed to context.

**Residual risk:** If RBAC grants impersonation permissions too broadly — e.g., `verbs: ["impersonate"], resources: ["users"], resourceNames: []` without restricting the target — an attacker can impersonate arbitrary users. Wildcards in impersonation RBAC rules are particularly dangerous.

**Absence of escalation guardrail at impersonation time:** The impersonation filter does not check whether the impersonated user would have more privileges than the real user. It only verifies the `impersonate` permission. An account with `impersonate` on `groups` and no restriction on `system:masters` could escalate to cluster-admin.

#### 3. Privilege Escalation via RBAC Policy Escalation

The RBAC admission layer includes `ConfirmNoEscalation` (`rule.go:53`): when a user creates or updates a Role or ClusterRole, the API validates that the resulting rules are a subset of the requesting user's own permissions. This prevents a user from granting themselves permissions they do not already hold via RBAC object manipulation.

**Residual risk:** The check is only as accurate as the rule resolver at admission time. If the shared informer cache has a delayed view of role bindings (stale cache), a user might be able to briefly escalate by racing between binding creation and cache synchronization, though this is highly impractical.

#### 4. Policy Misconfiguration (Medium Risk — Operational)

**Cluster-admin ClusterRoleBinding abuse:** The default bootstrap policy includes a `cluster-admin` ClusterRole bound to `system:masters`. Any certificate issued with `O=system:masters` is unconditionally authorized by the `privilegedGroupAuthorizer` at `reload.go:98` before RBAC is even consulted. This is a hardcoded bypass without audit-level identity granularity.

**Wildcard rules in RBAC:** PolicyRule wildcards (`verbs: ["*"]`, `resources: ["*"]`, `apiGroups: ["*"]`) grant blanket permissions. The `bootstrappolicy` deliberately grants these to `cluster-admin`, but if granted to end-user accounts, they provide full cluster access.

**RoleBinding to ClusterRole:** A RoleBinding can reference a ClusterRole, scoping the cluster-wide role to a single namespace. Conversely, ClusterRoleBindings apply cluster-wide. Misconfiguration (e.g., granting `cluster-admin` as a namespaced RoleBinding) may be less dangerous than intended, but granting it as a ClusterRoleBinding is full cluster compromise.

**Default service account token:** Every pod receives an auto-mounted service account token. The `default` service account in each namespace has no RBAC permissions by default, but if cluster operators have granted broad bindings to `system:authenticated` or to the `default` service account, pod compromise directly yields API access.

#### 5. Authorizer Chain Logic Flaw (Design-Level Consideration)

**RBAC never denies explicitly.** The `RBACAuthorizer.Authorize` method returns `DecisionNoOpinion` on denial (`rbac.go:126`). This means:
- If RBAC is listed before a Webhook authorizer and RBAC has no matching rule, the request moves to the webhook authorizer, which may allow it.
- The effective denial only occurs when the union authorizer exhausts all authorizers without an `Allow` or `Deny`, and the filter treats `NoOpinion` as a 403.

In a chain of `[Node, RBAC, Webhook]`, a request denied by RBAC still reaches the Webhook. If the Webhook is lenient, RBAC's lack of an explicit deny creates an allow path outside RBAC policy. Operators must understand that the chain is ordered and that `NoOpinion` propagates forward.

#### 6. Authorization Config Hot-Reload Attack Surface

`reload.go:185` (`runReload`) polls the authorization config file every minute. If the file on disk is writable by an attacker, they can inject a new authorizer configuration — for example, changing the chain to `AlwaysAllow` mode or inserting a malicious webhook endpoint. The file must be protected with appropriate OS-level permissions.

### Existing Mitigations

| Mitigation | Location | What It Prevents |
|------------|----------|------------------|
| Auth header clearing after authentication | `authentication.go:88–108` | Header injection / proxy spoofing |
| HTTP/2 GOAWAY for anonymous users | `authentication.go:115–120` | CVE-2023-44487 / HTTP/2 DoS |
| Impersonation requires explicit `impersonate` verb | `impersonation.go:117` | Unauthorized identity substitution |
| Impersonation headers cleared after substitution | `impersonation.go:173–180` | Stacked impersonation attacks |
| `ConfirmNoEscalation` at admission | `rule.go:53` | RBAC policy escalation via object manipulation |
| `privilegedGroupAuthorizer` is prepended to chain | `reload.go:98` | Ensures break-glass access via client certs |
| Informer cache for role/binding resolution | `rbac.go:199–224` | Avoids per-request etcd reads; provides consistency |
| Audit annotations on every decision | `authorization.go:80–95` | Forensic trail of allow/deny and reason |
| `instrumentedAuthorizer` metrics wrapper | `metrics/metrics.go:62` | Per-authorizer decision observability |
| Authorizer config validation on load | `config.go:163–213` | Prevents malformed config from replacing the chain |
| `DecisionDeny` short-circuits chain | `union.go:61` | Webhook or Node authorizer can explicitly block |

### Security Boundaries Between Authorization Plugins

The union authorizer (`union.go`) is the boundary manager. The interaction rules are:

- **`DecisionAllow`**: stops evaluation, request proceeds. The first authorizer to allow wins.
- **`DecisionDeny`**: stops evaluation, request is rejected immediately. Only Node and Webhook authorizers currently return explicit Deny; RBAC does not.
- **`DecisionNoOpinion`**: falls through to the next authorizer.

There is **no explicit isolation** between authorizers — they all receive the same `Attributes` object. A plugin cannot prevent a later plugin from allowing what it declined. The only true veto mechanism is `DecisionDeny`.

The `system:masters` bypass (`privilegedGroupAuthorizer`) is inserted **before all configured authorizers** in `newForConfig`, meaning even if RBAC or Webhook would deny the request, `system:masters` group membership preempts the check. This is intentional for emergency/bootstrapping scenarios but represents a permanent bypass with no policy-based override.

### Authorizer Chain Interaction (RBAC, Node, Webhook)

**Node authorizer** (`plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/node`) is purpose-built for kubelet identity. It uses a dependency graph (Pods → PVs → Secrets/ConfigMaps) to allow a node to only access secrets/configmaps used by pods scheduled on it. It returns explicit `DecisionDeny` for requests to resources a node should not access. The Node authorizer is positioned in the chain before RBAC and can block requests that RBAC might otherwise allow.

**RBAC authorizer** evaluates policy rules from the etcd-backed informer cache. It is purely additive (no explicit deny). It covers the bulk of cluster authorization for human users and service accounts.

**Webhook authorizer** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/authorizer/webhook`) sends a `SubjectAccessReview` to an external HTTPS endpoint. It supports response caching (allow TTL, deny TTL), retry backoff, and CEL-based match conditions to filter which requests reach the webhook. The `FailurePolicy` (NoOpinion or Deny) determines behavior when the webhook is unreachable — a critical operational security decision.

---

## Summary

The Kubernetes RBAC authorization subsystem implements a **defense-in-depth, additive-allow model** with a clearly ordered HTTP filter chain. The key security properties are:

1. **Authentication precedes authorization** and headers that could influence identity are purged after authentication, limiting header injection scope.

2. **Impersonation is independently authorized** against the real user's identity before the substituted identity is committed. However, over-broad impersonation RBAC grants remain the most practical privilege escalation path.

3. **RBAC never explicitly denies** — it returns `DecisionNoOpinion`. Explicit denials are only possible from the Node or Webhook authorizers. Operators who expect RBAC to block requests should understand that later authorizers in the chain may grant access if RBAC has no matching rule.

4. **The `system:masters` group is a hardcoded, unconditional bypass** inserted at the front of the authorizer chain. No RBAC policy can block a request from a member of `system:masters`. This group should be treated with the same sensitivity as root credentials.

5. **Policy data is served from an in-memory informer cache** (not direct etcd reads per request), which provides performance and read consistency but introduces a brief window during which RBAC changes may not yet be reflected in authorization decisions.

6. **Audit logging is integrated at multiple points** — authentication failure, impersonation events, and authorization decisions all produce structured audit entries, providing a forensic trail. However, the audit system is optional; if no backend is configured, `WithAudit` degrades to a no-op.

7. **The authorizer chain is hot-reloadable** from a file, which introduces a file-system trust dependency. The reload file must be protected against unauthorized writes.

The most actionable security risks are:
- **Misconfigured impersonation RBAC** (widest practical escalation path)
- **AlwaysAllow mode** enabled inadvertently (complete bypass)
- **Overly broad wildcard rules** in ClusterRoleBindings targeting `system:authenticated`
- **Unprotected authorization config file** enabling chain substitution
- **Stale informer cache** creating a brief inconsistency window after policy changes (low practical risk, no known exploit path)

conversation_events.text:
# Kubernetes RBAC Authorization Flow Security Audit

**Repository:** kubernetes/kubernetes
**Task Type:** Security Analysis (analysis only — no code changes)

## Scenario

You are a security engineer auditing the Kubernetes API server's RBAC authorization subsystem. Your goal is to trace the complete authorization flow from when an API request arrives to when a permission decision (allow/deny) is made, mapping the attack surface and identifying security controls.

## Your Task

Analyze the Kubernetes RBAC authorization flow and write a comprehensive security analysis. Trace how API requests are authorized, identify entry points where untrusted input enters the authorization decision path, map the data flow through authorization plugins, and assess the security posture of the RBAC implementation.

Write your analysis to `/logs/agent/solution.md`.

## Required Sections

### Files Examined
- List each file you analyzed and its role in the RBAC authorization flow

### Entry Points
1. Identify where API requests enter the authorization subsystem
2. Identify where RBAC policy data (roles, bindings) enters the system
3. Note any places where untrusted input could influence authorization decisions

### Data Flow
Trace the authorization flow through these stages:
1. **Request arrival** — HTTP handler receives the request
2. **Authentication** — Request identity is established
3. **Authorization filter** — Request is routed to authorizer chain
4. **RBAC evaluation** — Role and binding resolution, permission check
5. **Decision** — Allow/deny outcome and audit logging

For each stage, name the specific file, function, and data structures involved.

### Dependency Chain
Provide an ordered list of files from request entry to authorization decision.

### Analysis
- What vulnerability classes could apply (auth bypass, privilege escalation, policy misconfiguration)?
- What existing mitigations are in place?
- What are the security boundaries between authorization plugins?
- How does the authorizer chain (RBAC, Node, Webhook) interact?

### Summary
Concise description of the RBAC authorization security posture and key findings.

## Constraints

- **Analysis only** — do NOT modify any source files
- Be specific — include file paths, function names, and type names
- Focus on the `plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/` and `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/` directories
Let me start by exploring the relevant directories to understand the codebase structure.
Now let me read the key files in parallel to understand the authorization flow:
Now I have enough information to write the comprehensive security analysis. Let me write it:

The security analysis has been written to `/logs/agent/solution.md`. Here is a summary of the key findings:

---

## Analysis Summary

### Authorization Flow (in execution order)

```
Request → WithRequestInfo → WithAuthentication → WithAudit
        → WithImpersonation → WithAuthorization → API Handler
```

The chain is assembled in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:1004` (`DefaultBuildHandlerChain`), constructed in reverse registration order.

### Critical Security Findings

**1. `system:masters` is an unconditional hardcoded bypass** (`reload.go:98`). The `privilegedGroupAuthorizer` is prepended to the authorizer chain before RBAC, Node, or Webhook authorizers run. Any request from a member of `system:masters` is allowed without any policy check — no RBAC rule can block it.

**2. RBAC never explicitly denies** (`rbac.go:126`). `RBACAuthorizer.Authorize` returns `DecisionNoOpinion` on failure, not `DecisionDeny`. In a chain of `[RBAC, Webhook]`, a request not covered by any RBAC rule still reaches the Webhook, which may allow it. Explicit denial is only possible from the Node or Webhook authorizers.

**3. Impersonation is the primary privilege escalation vector** (`impersonation.go:117`). The `WithImpersonation` filter runs *before* `WithAuthorization`. If a user holds the `impersonate` verb on `groups` with no `resourceNames` restriction, they can impersonate `system:masters` and bypass all downstream policy.

**4. Policy data comes from an async informer cache**, not etcd. There is a brief consistency window after RBAC policy changes where the old policy is still in effect.

**5. Authorization config hot-reload** (`reload.go:185`) polls a file on disk every minute. If that file is writable by an attacker, the entire authorizer chain can be replaced (e.g., with `AlwaysAllow`).

**Key mitigations in place:** proxy header clearing after authentication (prevents header injection), `ConfirmNoEscalation` at RBAC admission time (prevents policy escalation), HTTP/2 DoS mitigation for anonymous users (CVE-2023-44487), per-authorizer decision metrics, and structured audit annotations on every authorization outcome.

    23→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields"
    24→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels"
    25→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
    26→)
    27→
    28→// Attributes is an interface used by an Authorizer to get information about a request
    29→// that is used to make an authorization decision.
    30→type Attributes interface {
    31→	// GetUser returns the user.Info object to authorize
    32→	GetUser() user.Info
    33→
    34→	// GetVerb returns the kube verb associated with API requests (this includes get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, deletecollection, and proxy),
    35→	// or the lowercased HTTP verb associated with non-API requests (this includes get, put, post, patch, and delete)
    36→	GetVerb() string
    37→
    38→	// When IsReadOnly() == true, the request has no side effects, other than
    39→	// caching, logging, and other incidentals.
    40→	IsReadOnly() bool
    41→
    42→	// The namespace of the object, if a request is for a REST object.
    43→	GetNamespace() string
    44→
    45→	// The kind of object, if a request is for a REST object.
    46→	GetResource() string
    47→
    48→	// GetSubresource returns the subresource being requested, if present
    49→	GetSubresource() string
    50→
    51→	// GetName returns the name of the object as parsed off the request.  This will not be present for all request types, but
    52→	// will be present for: get, update, delete
    53→	GetName() string
    54→
    55→	// The group of the resource, if a request is for a REST object.
    56→	GetAPIGroup() string
    57→
    58→	// GetAPIVersion returns the version of the group requested, if a request is for a REST object.
    59→	GetAPIVersion() string
    60→
    61→	// IsResourceRequest returns true for requests to API resources, like /api/v1/nodes,
    62→	// and false for non-resource endpoints like /api, /healthz
    63→	IsResourceRequest() bool
    64→
    65→	// GetPath returns the path of the request
    66→	GetPath() string
    67→
    68→	// ParseFieldSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.
    69→	// It returns an error if the field selector cannot be parsed.
    70→	// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.
    71→	GetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error)
    72→
    73→	// ParseLabelSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.
    74→	// It returns an error if the label selector cannot be parsed.
    75→	// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.
    76→	GetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error)
    77→}
    78→
    79→// Authorizer makes an authorization decision based on information gained by making
    80→// zero or more calls to methods of the Attributes interface.  It returns nil when an action is
    81→// authorized, otherwise it returns an error.
    82→type Authorizer interface {
    83→	Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (authorized Decision, reason string, err error)
    84→}
    85→
    86→type AuthorizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error)
    87→
    88→func (f AuthorizerFunc) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error) {
    89→	return f(ctx, a)
    90→}
    91→
    92→// RuleResolver provides a mechanism for resolving the list of rules that apply to a given user within a namespace.
    93→type RuleResolver interface {
    94→	// RulesFor get the list of cluster wide rules, the list of rules in the specific namespace, incomplete status and errors.
    95→	RulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]ResourceRuleInfo, []NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error)
    96→}
    97→
    98→// RequestAttributesGetter provides a function that extracts Attributes from an http.Request
    99→type RequestAttributesGetter interface {
   100→	GetRequestAttributes(user.Info, *http.Request) Attributes
   101→}
   102→
   103→// AttributesRecord implements Attributes interface.
   104→type AttributesRecord struct {
   105→	User            user.Info
   106→	Verb            string
   107→	Namespace       string
   108→	APIGroup        string
   109→	APIVersion      string
   110→	Resource        string
   111→	Subresource     string
   112→	Name            string
   113→	ResourceRequest bool
   114→	Path            string
   115→
   116→	FieldSelectorRequirements fields.Requirements
   117→	FieldSelectorParsingErr   error
   118→	LabelSelectorRequirements labels.Requirements
   119→	LabelSelectorParsingErr   error
   120→}
   121→
   122→func (a AttributesRecord) GetUser() user.Info {
   123→	return a.User
   124→}
   125→
   126→func (a AttributesRecord) GetVerb() string {
   127→	return a.Verb
   128→}
   129→
   130→func (a AttributesRecord) IsReadOnly() bool {
   131→	return a.Verb == "get" || a.Verb == "list" || a.Verb == "watch"
   132→}
   133→
   134→func (a AttributesRecord) GetNamespace() string {
   135→	return a.Namespace
   136→}
   137→
   138→func (a AttributesRecord) GetResource() string {
   139→	return a.Resource
   140→}
   141→
   142→func (a AttributesRecord) GetSubresource() string {
   143→	return a.Subresource
   144→}
   145→
   146→func (a AttributesRecord) GetName() string {
   147→	return a.Name
   148→}
   149→
   150→func (a AttributesRecord) GetAPIGroup() string {
   151→	return a.APIGroup
   152→}
   153→
   154→func (a AttributesRecord) GetAPIVersion() string {
   155→	return a.APIVersion
   156→}
   157→
   158→func (a AttributesRecord) IsResourceRequest() bool {
   159→	return a.ResourceRequest
   160→}
   161→
   162→func (a AttributesRecord) GetPath() string {
   163→	return a.Path
   164→}
   165→
   166→func (a AttributesRecord) GetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error) {
   167→	return a.FieldSelectorRequirements, a.FieldSelectorParsingErr
   168→}
   169→
   170→func (a AttributesRecord) GetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error) {
   171→	return a.LabelSelectorRequirements, a.LabelSelectorParsingErr
   172→}
   173→
   174→type Decision int
   175→
   176→const (
   177→	// DecisionDeny means that an authorizer decided to deny the action.
   178→	DecisionDeny Decision = iota
   179→	// DecisionAllow means that an authorizer decided to allow the action.
   180→	DecisionAllow
   181→	// DecisionNoOpinion means that an authorizer has no opinion on whether
   182→	// to allow or deny an action.
   183→	DecisionNoOpinion
   184→)
   185→

    17→package filters
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"errors"
    22→	"net/http"
    23→	"time"
    24→
    25→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields"
    26→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels"
    27→	genericfeatures "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features"
    28→	utilfeature "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature"
    29→
    30→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    31→
    32→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    33→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit"
    34→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer"
    35→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters"
    36→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"
    37→)
    38→
    39→const (
    40→	// Annotation key names set in advanced audit
    41→	decisionAnnotationKey = "authorization.k8s.io/decision"
    42→	reasonAnnotationKey   = "authorization.k8s.io/reason"
    43→
    44→	// Annotation values set in advanced audit
    45→	decisionAllow  = "allow"
    46→	decisionForbid = "forbid"
    47→	reasonError    = "internal error"
    48→)
    49→
    50→type recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc func(ctx context.Context, authorized authorizer.Decision, err error, authStart time.Time, authFinish time.Time)
    51→
    52→// WithAuthorization passes all authorized requests on to handler, and returns a forbidden error otherwise.
    53→func WithAuthorization(hhandler http.Handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {
    54→	return withAuthorization(hhandler, auth, s, recordAuthorizationMetrics)
    55→}
    56→
    57→func withAuthorization(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, metrics recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc) http.Handler {
    58→	if a == nil {
    59→		klog.Warning("Authorization is disabled")
    60→		return handler
    61→	}
    62→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    63→		ctx := req.Context()
    64→		authorizationStart := time.Now()
    65→
    66→		attributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)
    67→		if err != nil {
    68→			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)
    69→			return
    70→		}
    71→		authorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)
    72→
    73→		authorizationFinish := time.Now()
    74→		defer func() {
    75→			metrics(ctx, authorized, err, authorizationStart, authorizationFinish)
    76→		}()
    77→
    78→		// an authorizer like RBAC could encounter evaluation errors and still allow the request, so authorizer decision is checked before error here.
    79→		if authorized == authorizer.DecisionAllow {
    80→			audit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,
    81→				decisionAnnotationKey, decisionAllow,
    82→				reasonAnnotationKey, reason)
    83→			handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    84→			return
    85→		}
    86→		if err != nil {
    87→			audit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, reasonAnnotationKey, reasonError)
    88→			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)
    89→			return
    90→		}
    91→
    92→		klog.V(4).InfoS("Forbidden", "URI", req.RequestURI, "reason", reason)
    93→		audit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,
    94→			decisionAnnotationKey, decisionForbid,
    95→			reasonAnnotationKey, reason)
    96→		responsewriters.Forbidden(ctx, attributes, w, req, reason, s)
    97→	})
    98→}
    99→
   100→func GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx context.Context) (authorizer.Attributes, error) {
   101→	attribs := authorizer.AttributesRecord{}
   102→
   103→	user, ok := request.UserFrom(ctx)
   104→	if ok {
   105→		attribs.User = user
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	requestInfo, found := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)
   109→	if !found {
   110→		return nil, errors.New("no RequestInfo found in the context")
   111→	}
   112→
   113→	// Start with common attributes that apply to resource and non-resource requests
   114→	attribs.ResourceRequest = requestInfo.IsResourceRequest
   115→	attribs.Path = requestInfo.Path
   116→	attribs.Verb = requestInfo.Verb
   117→
   118→	attribs.APIGroup = requestInfo.APIGroup
   119→	attribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion
   120→	attribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource
   121→	attribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource
   122→	attribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace
   123→	attribs.Name = requestInfo.Name
   124→
   125→	if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {
   126→		// parsing here makes it easy to keep the AttributesRecord type value-only and avoids any mutex copies when
   127→		// doing shallow copies in other steps.
   128→		if len(requestInfo.FieldSelector) > 0 {
   129→			fieldSelector, err := fields.ParseSelector(requestInfo.FieldSelector)
   130→			if err != nil {
   131→				attribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = nil, err
   132→			} else {
   133→				if requirements := fieldSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {
   134→					attribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = fieldSelector.Requirements(), nil
   135→				}
   136→			}
   137→		}
   138→
   139→		if len(requestInfo.LabelSelector) > 0 {
   140→			labelSelector, err := labels.Parse(requestInfo.LabelSelector)
   141→			if err != nil {
   142→				attribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = nil, err
   143→			} else {
   144→				if requirements, _ /*selectable*/ := labelSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {
   145→					attribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = requirements, nil
   146→				}
   147→			}
   148→		}
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	return &attribs, nil
   152→}
   153→

requestInfo.APIGroup\n\tattribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion\n\tattribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource\n\tattribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource\n\tattribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace\n\tattribs.Name = requestInfo.Name\n\n\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {\n\t\t// parsing here makes it easy to keep the     17→package filters
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"errors"
    22→	"fmt"
    23→	"net/http"
    24→	"time"
    25→
    26→	apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
    27→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    28→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
    29→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator"
    30→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory"
    31→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest"
    32→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
    33→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters"
    34→	genericapirequest "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"
    35→	genericfeatures "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features"
    36→	utilfeature "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature"
    37→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    38→)
    39→
    40→type authenticationRecordMetricsFunc func(context.Context, *authenticator.Response, bool, error, authenticator.Audiences, time.Time, time.Time)
    41→
    42→// WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then
    43→// stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error
    44→// the failed handler is used. On success, "Authorization" header is removed from the request and handler
    45→// is invoked to serve the request.
    46→func WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {
    47→	return withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)
    48→}
    49→
    50→func withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {
    51→	if auth == nil {
    52→		klog.Warning("Authentication is disabled")
    53→		return handler
    54→	}
    55→	standardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{
    56→		UsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-User"},
    57→		UIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Uid"},
    58→		GroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Group"},
    59→		ExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Extra-"},
    60→	}
    61→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    62→		authenticationStart := time.Now()
    63→
    64→		if len(apiAuds) > 0 {
    65→			req = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))
    66→		}
    67→		resp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)
    68→		authenticationFinish := time.Now()
    69→		defer func() {
    70→			metrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)
    71→		}()
    72→		if err != nil || !ok {
    73→			if err != nil {
    74→				klog.ErrorS(err, "Unable to authenticate the request")
    75→			}
    76→			failed.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    77→			return
    78→		}
    79→
    80→		if !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {
    81→			err = fmt.Errorf("unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)
    82→			klog.Error(err)
    83→			failed.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    84→			return
    85→		}
    86→
    87→		// authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.
    88→		req.Header.Del("Authorization")
    89→
    90→		// delete standard front proxy headers
    91→		headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(
    92→			req.Header,
    93→			standardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
    94→			standardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
    95→			standardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
    96→			standardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
    97→		)
    98→
    99→		// also delete any custom front proxy headers
   100→		if requestHeaderConfig != nil {
   101→			headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(
   102→				req.Header,
   103→				requestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
   104→				requestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
   105→				requestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
   106→				requestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
   107→			)
   108→		}
   109→
   110→		// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,
   111→		// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.
   112→		// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these
   113→		// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the
   114→		// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).
   115→		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 && isAnonymousUser(resp.User) {
   116→			// limit this connection to just this request,
   117→			// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection
   118→			// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c
   119→			w.Header().Set("Connection", "close")
   120→		}
   121→
   122→		req = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))
   123→		handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
   124→	})
   125→}
   126→
   127→func Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {
   128→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
   129→		// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,
   130→		// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.
   131→		// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these
   132→		// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the
   133→		// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).
   134→		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {
   135→			// limit this connection to just this request,
   136→			// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection
   137→			// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c
   138→			w.Header().Set("Connection", "close")
   139→		}
   140→		ctx := req.Context()
   141→		requestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)
   142→		if !found {
   143→			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New("no RequestInfo found in the context"))
   144→			return
   145→		}
   146→
   147→		gv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}
   148→		responsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized("Unauthorized"), s, gv, w, req)
   149→	})
   150→}
   151→
   152→func audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {
   153→	if len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {
   154→		return true
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	return len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0
   158→}
   159→
   160→func isAnonymousUser(u user.Info) bool {
   161→	if u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {
   162→		return true
   163→	}
   164→	for _, group := range u.GetGroups() {
   165→		if group == user.AllUnauthenticated {
   166→			return true
   167→		}
   168→	}
   169→	return false
   170→}
   171→

    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.

    16→
    17→// Package union implements an authorizer that combines multiple subauthorizer.
    18→// The union authorizer iterates over each subauthorizer and returns the first
    19→// decision that is either an Allow decision or a Deny decision. If a
    20→// subauthorizer returns a NoOpinion, then the union authorizer moves onto the
    21→// next authorizer or, if the subauthorizer was the last authorizer, returns
    22→// NoOpinion as the aggregate decision. I.e. union authorizer creates an
    23→// aggregate decision and supports short-circuit allows and denies from
    24→// subauthorizers.
    25→package union
    26→
    27→import (
    28→	"context"
    29→	"strings"
    30→
    31→	utilerrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors"
    32→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
    33→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer"
    34→)
    35→
    36→// unionAuthzHandler authorizer against a chain of authorizer.Authorizer
    37→type unionAuthzHandler []authorizer.Authorizer
    38→
    39→// New returns an authorizer that authorizes against a chain of authorizer.Authorizer objects
    40→func New(authorizationHandlers ...authorizer.Authorizer) authorizer.Authorizer {
    41→	return unionAuthzHandler(authorizationHandlers)
    42→}
    43→
    44→// Authorizes against a chain of authorizer.Authorizer objects and returns nil if successful and returns error if unsuccessful
    45→func (authzHandler unionAuthzHandler) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a authorizer.Attributes) (authorizer.Decision, string, error) {
    46→	var (
    47→		errlist    []error
    48→		reasonlist []string
    49→	)
    50→
    51→	for _, currAuthzHandler := range authzHandler {
    52→		decision, reason, err := currAuthzHandler.Authorize(ctx, a)
    53→
    54→		if err != nil {
    55→			errlist = append(errlist, err)
    56→		}
    57→		if len(reason) != 0 {
    58→			reasonlist = append(reasonlist, reason)
    59→		}
    60→		switch decision {
    61→		case authorizer.DecisionAllow, authorizer.DecisionDeny:
    62→			return decision, reason, err
    63→		case authorizer.DecisionNoOpinion:
    64→			// continue to the next authorizer
    65→		}
    66→	}
    67→
    68→	return authorizer.DecisionNoOpinion, strings.Join(reasonlist, "\n"), utilerrors.NewAggregate(errlist)
    69→}
    70→
    71→// unionAuthzRulesHandler authorizer against a chain of authorizer.RuleResolver
    72→type unionAuthzRulesHandler []authorizer.RuleResolver
    73→
    74→// NewRuleResolvers returns an authorizer that authorizes against a chain of authorizer.Authorizer objects
    75→func NewRuleResolvers(authorizationHandlers ...authorizer.RuleResolver) authorizer.RuleResolver {
    76→	return unionAuthzRulesHandler(authorizationHandlers)
    77→}
    78→
    79→// RulesFor against a chain of authorizer.RuleResolver objects and returns nil if successful and returns error if unsuccessful
    80→func (authzHandler unionAuthzRulesHandler) RulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]authorizer.ResourceRuleInfo, []authorizer.NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error) {
    81→	var (
    82→		errList              []error
    83→		resourceRulesList    []authorizer.ResourceRuleInfo
    84→		nonResourceRulesList []authorizer.NonResourceRuleInfo
    85→	)
    86→	incompleteStatus := false
    87→
    88→	for _, currAuthzHandler := range authzHandler {
    89→		resourceRules, nonResourceRules, incomplete, err := currAuthzHandler.RulesFor(ctx, user, namespace)
    90→
    91→		if incomplete {
    92→			incompleteStatus = true
    93→		}
    94→		if err != nil {
    95→			errList = append(errList, err)
    96→		}
    97→		if len(resourceRules) > 0 {
    98→			resourceRulesList = append(resourceRulesList, resourceRules...)
    99→		}
   100→		if len(nonResourceRules) > 0 {
   101→			nonResourceRulesList = append(nonResourceRulesList, nonResourceRules...)
   102→		}
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	return resourceRulesList, nonResourceRulesList, incompleteStatus, utilerrors.NewAggregate(errList)
   106→}
   107→

and returns error if unsuccessful\nfunc (authzHandler unionAuthzHandler) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a authorizer.Attributes) (authorizer.Decision, string, error) {\n\tvar (\n\t\terrlist    []error\n\t\treasonlist []string\n\t)\n\n\tfor _, currAuthzHandler := range authzHandler {\n\t\tdecision, reason, err := currAuthzHandler.Authorize(ctx, a)\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrlist = append(errlist, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(reason) != 0 {\n\t\t\treasonlist = append(reasonlist, reason)\n\t\t}\n\t\tswitch decision {\n\t\tcase authorizer.DecisionAllow, authorizer.DecisionDeny:\n\t\t\treturn decision, reason, err\n\t\tcase authorizer.DecisionNoOpinion:\n\t\t\t// continue to the next authorizer\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn authorizer.DecisionNoOpinion, strings.Join(reasonlist, \"\\n\"), utilerrors.NewAggregate(errlist)\n}\n\n// unionAuthzRulesHandler authorizer against a chain of authorizer.RuleResolver\ntype unionAuthzRulesHandler []authorizer.RuleResolver\n\n// NewRuleResolvers returns an authorizer that authorizes against a chain of authorizer.Authorizer objects\nfunc NewRuleResolvers(authorizationHandlers ...authorizer.RuleResolver) authorizer.RuleResolver {\n\treturn unionAuthzRulesHandler(authorizationHandlers)\n}\n\n// RulesFor against a chain of authorizer.RuleResolver objects and returns nil if successful and returns error if unsuccessful\nfunc (authzHandler unionAuthzRulesHandler) RulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]authorizer.ResourceRuleInfo, []authorizer.NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error) {\n\tvar (\n\t\terrList              []error\n\t\tresourceRulesList    []authorizer.ResourceRuleInfo\n\t\tnonResourceRulesList []authorizer.NonResourceRuleInfo\n\t)\n\tincompleteStatus := false\n\n\tfor _, currAuthzHandler := range authzHandler {\n\t\tresourceRules, nonResourceRules, incomplete, err := currAuthzHandler.RulesFor(ctx, user, namespace)\n\n\t\tif incomplete {\n\t\t\tincompleteStatus = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrList = append(errList, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(resourceRules) > 0 {\n\t\t\tresourceRulesList = append(resourceRulesList, resourceRules...)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(nonResourceRules) > 0 {\n\t\t\tnonResourceRulesList = append(nonResourceRulesList, nonResourceRules...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn resourceRulesList, nonResourceRulesList, incompleteStatus, utilerrors.NewAggregate(errList)\n}\n", "numLines": 107, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 107}}

    17→package filters
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"errors"
    21→	"fmt"
    22→	"net/http"
    23→	"net/url"
    24→	"strings"
    25→
    26→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    27→
    28→	authenticationv1 "k8s.io/api/authentication/v1"
    29→	"k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    30→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    31→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit"
    32→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/serviceaccount"
    33→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
    34→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer"
    35→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters"
    36→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"
    37→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/httplog"
    38→)
    39→
    40→// WithImpersonation is a filter that will inspect and check requests that attempt to change the user.Info for their requests
    41→func WithImpersonation(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {
    42→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    43→		impersonationRequests, err := buildImpersonationRequests(req.Header)
    44→		if err != nil {
    45→			klog.V(4).Infof("%v", err)
    46→			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)
    47→			return
    48→		}
    49→		if len(impersonationRequests) == 0 {
    50→			handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    51→			return
    52→		}
    53→
    54→		ctx := req.Context()
    55→		requestor, exists := request.UserFrom(ctx)
    56→		if !exists {
    57→			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New("no user found for request"))
    58→			return
    59→		}
    60→
    61→		// if groups are not specified, then we need to look them up differently depending on the type of user
    62→		// if they are specified, then they are the authority (including the inclusion of system:authenticated/system:unauthenticated groups)
    63→		groupsSpecified := len(req.Header[authenticationv1.ImpersonateGroupHeader]) > 0
    64→
    65→		// make sure we're allowed to impersonate each thing we're requesting.  While we're iterating through, start building username
    66→		// and group information
    67→		username := ""
    68→		groups := []string{}
    69→		userExtra := map[string][]string{}
    70→		uid := ""
    71→		for _, impersonationRequest := range impersonationRequests {
    72→			gvk := impersonationRequest.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()
    73→			actingAsAttributes := &authorizer.AttributesRecord{
    74→				User:            requestor,
    75→				Verb:            "impersonate",
    76→				APIGroup:        gvk.Group,
    77→				APIVersion:      gvk.Version,
    78→				Namespace:       impersonationRequest.Namespace,
    79→				Name:            impersonationRequest.Name,
    80→				ResourceRequest: true,
    81→			}
    82→
    83→			switch gvk.GroupKind() {
    84→			case v1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("ServiceAccount").GroupKind():
    85→				actingAsAttributes.Resource = "serviceaccounts"
    86→				username = serviceaccount.MakeUsername(impersonationRequest.Namespace, impersonationRequest.Name)
    87→				if !groupsSpecified {
    88→					// if groups aren't specified for a service account, we know the groups because its a fixed mapping.  Add them
    89→					groups = serviceaccount.MakeGroupNames(impersonationRequest.Namespace)
    90→				}
    91→
    92→			case v1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("User").GroupKind():
    93→				actingAsAttributes.Resource = "users"
    94→				username = impersonationRequest.Name
    95→
    96→			case v1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("Group").GroupKind():
    97→				actingAsAttributes.Resource = "groups"
    98→				groups = append(groups, impersonationRequest.Name)
    99→
   100→			case authenticationv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("UserExtra").GroupKind():
   101→				extraKey := impersonationRequest.FieldPath
   102→				extraValue := impersonationRequest.Name
   103→				actingAsAttributes.Resource = "userextras"
   104→				actingAsAttributes.Subresource = extraKey
   105→				userExtra[extraKey] = append(userExtra[extraKey], extraValue)
   106→
   107→			case authenticationv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("UID").GroupKind():
   108→				uid = string(impersonationRequest.Name)
   109→				actingAsAttributes.Resource = "uids"
   110→
   111→			default:
   112→				klog.V(4).InfoS("unknown impersonation request type", "request", impersonationRequest)
   113→				responsewriters.Forbidden(ctx, actingAsAttributes, w, req, fmt.Sprintf("unknown impersonation request type: %v", impersonationRequest), s)
   114→				return
   115→			}
   116→
   117→			decision, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, actingAsAttributes)
   118→			if err != nil || decision != authorizer.DecisionAllow {
   119→				klog.V(4).InfoS("Forbidden", "URI", req.RequestURI, "reason", reason, "err", err)
   120→				responsewriters.Forbidden(ctx, actingAsAttributes, w, req, reason, s)
   121→				return
   122→			}
   123→		}
   124→
   125→		if username != user.Anonymous {
   126→			// When impersonating a non-anonymous user, include the 'system:authenticated' group
   127→			// in the impersonated user info:
   128→			// - if no groups were specified
   129→			// - if a group has been specified other than 'system:authenticated'
   130→			//
   131→			// If 'system:unauthenticated' group has been specified we should not include
   132→			// the 'system:authenticated' group.
   133→			addAuthenticated := true
   134→			for _, group := range groups {
   135→				if group == user.AllAuthenticated || group == user.AllUnauthenticated {
   136→					addAuthenticated = false
   137→					break
   138→				}
   139→			}
   140→
   141→			if addAuthenticated {
   142→				groups = append(groups, user.AllAuthenticated)
   143→			}
   144→		} else {
   145→			addUnauthenticated := true
   146→			for _, group := range groups {
   147→				if group == user.AllUnauthenticated {
   148→					addUnauthenticated = false
   149→					break
   150→				}
   151→			}
   152→
   153→			if addUnauthenticated {
   154→				groups = append(groups, user.AllUnauthenticated)
   155→			}
   156→		}
   157→
   158→		newUser := &user.DefaultInfo{
   159→			Name:   username,
   160→			Groups: groups,
   161→			Extra:  userExtra,
   162→			UID:    uid,
   163→		}
   164→		req = req.WithContext(request.WithUser(ctx, newUser))
   165→
   166→		oldUser, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)
   167→		httplog.LogOf(req, w).Addf("%v is impersonating %v", userString(oldUser), userString(newUser))
   168→
   169→		ae := audit.AuditEventFrom(ctx)
   170→		audit.LogImpersonatedUser(ae, newUser)
   171→
   172→		// clear all the impersonation headers from the request
   173→		req.Header.Del(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserHeader)
   174→		req.Header.Del(authenticationv1.ImpersonateGroupHeader)
   175→		req.Header.Del(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUIDHeader)
   176→		for headerName := range req.Header {
   177→			if strings.HasPrefix(headerName, authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserExtraHeaderPrefix) {
   178→				req.Header.Del(headerName)
   179→			}
   180→		}
   181→
   182→		handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
   183→	})
   184→}
   185→
   186→func userString(u user.Info) string {
   187→	if u == nil {
   188→		return "<none>"
   189→	}
   190→	b := strings.Builder{}
   191→	if name := u.GetName(); name == "" {
   192→		b.WriteString("<empty>")
   193→	} else {
   194→		b.WriteString(name)
   195→	}
   196→	if groups := u.GetGroups(); len(groups) > 0 {
   197→		b.WriteString("[")
   198→		b.WriteString(strings.Join(groups, ","))
   199→		b.WriteString("]")
   200→	}
   201→	return b.String()
   202→}
   203→
   204→func unescapeExtraKey(encodedKey string) string {
   205→	key, err := url.PathUnescape(encodedKey) // Decode %-encoded bytes.
   206→	if err != nil {
   207→		return encodedKey // Always record extra strings, even if malformed/unencoded.
   208→	}
   209→	return key
   210→}
   211→
   212→// buildImpersonationRequests returns a list of objectreferences that represent the different things we're requesting to impersonate.
   213→// Also includes a map[string][]string representing user.Info.Extra
   214→// Each request must be authorized against the current user before switching contexts.
   215→func buildImpersonationRequests(headers http.Header) ([]v1.ObjectReference, error) {
   216→	impersonationRequests := []v1.ObjectReference{}
   217→
   218→	requestedUser := headers.Get(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserHeader)
   219→	hasUser := len(requestedUser) > 0
   220→	if hasUser {
   221→		if namespace, name, err := serviceaccount.SplitUsername(requestedUser); err == nil {
   222→			impersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{Kind: "ServiceAccount", Namespace: namespace, Name: name})
   223→		} else {
   224→			impersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{Kind: "User", Name: requestedUser})
   225→		}
   226→	}
   227→
   228→	hasGroups := false
   229→	for _, group := range headers[authenticationv1.ImpersonateGroupHeader] {
   230→		hasGroups = true
   231→		impersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{Kind: "Group", Name: group})
   232→	}
   233→
   234→	hasUserExtra := false
   235→	for headerName, values := range headers {
   236→		if !strings.HasPrefix(headerName, authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserExtraHeaderPrefix) {
   237→			continue
   238→		}
   239→
   240→		hasUserExtra = true
   241→		extraKey := unescapeExtraKey(strings.ToLower(headerName[len(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserExtraHeaderPrefix):]))
   242→
   243→		// make a separate request for each extra value they're trying to set
   244→		for _, value := range values {
   245→			impersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests,
   246→				v1.ObjectReference{
   247→					Kind: "UserExtra",
   248→					// we only parse out a group above, but the parsing will fail if there isn't SOME version
   249→					// using the internal version will help us fail if anyone starts using it
   250→					APIVersion: authenticationv1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),
   251→					Name:       value,
   252→					// ObjectReference doesn't have a subresource field.  FieldPath is close and available, so we'll use that
   253→					// TODO fight the good fight for ObjectReference to refer to resources and subresources
   254→					FieldPath: extraKey,
   255→				})
   256→		}
   257→	}
   258→
   259→	requestedUID := headers.Get(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUIDHeader)
   260→	hasUID := len(requestedUID) > 0
   261→	if hasUID {
   262→		impersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{
   263→			Kind:       "UID",
   264→			Name:       requestedUID,
   265→			APIVersion: authenticationv1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),
   266→		})
   267→	}
   268→
   269→	if (hasGroups || hasUserExtra || hasUID) && !hasUser {
   270→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("requested %v without impersonating a user", impersonationRequests)
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	return impersonationRequests, nil
   274→}
   275→

url.PathUnescape(encodedKey) // Decode %-encoded bytes.\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn encodedKey // Always record extra strings, even if malformed/unencoded.\n\t}\n\treturn key\n}\n\n// buildImpersonationRequests returns a list of objectreferences that represent the different things we're requesting to impersonate.\n// Also includes a map[string][]string representing user.Info.Extra\n// Each request must be authorized against the current user before switching contexts.\nfunc buildImpersonationRequests(headers http.Header) ([]v1.ObjectReference, error) {\n\timpersonationRequests := []v1.ObjectReference{}\n\n\trequestedUser := headers.Get(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserHeader)\n\thasUser := len(requestedUser) > 0\n\tif hasUser {\n\t\tif namespace, name, err := serviceaccount.SplitUsername(requestedUser); err == nil {\n\t\t\timpersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"ServiceAccount\", Namespace: namespace, Name: name})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\timpersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"User\", Name: requestedUser})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\thasGroups := false\n\tfor _, group := range headers[authenticationv1.ImpersonateGroupHeader] {\n\t\thasGroups = true\n\t\timpersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Group\", Name: group})\n\t}\n\n\thasUserExtra := false\n\tfor headerName, values := range headers {\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(headerName, authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserExtraHeaderPrefix) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thasUserExtra = true\n\t\textraKey := unescapeExtraKey(strings.ToLower(headerName[len(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUserExtraHeaderPrefix):]))\n\n\t\t// make a separate request for each extra value they're trying to set\n\t\tfor _, value := range values {\n\t\t\timpersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests,\n\t\t\t\tv1.ObjectReference{\n\t\t\t\t\tKind: \"UserExtra\",\n\t\t\t\t\t// we only parse out a group above, but the parsing will fail if there isn't SOME version\n\t\t\t\t\t// using the internal version will help us fail if anyone starts using it\n\t\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: authenticationv1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tName:       value,\n\t\t\t\t\t// ObjectReference doesn't have a subresource field.  FieldPath is close and available, so we'll use that\n\t\t\t\t\t// TODO fight the good fight for ObjectReference to refer to resources and subresources\n\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath: extraKey,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\trequestedUID := headers.Get(authenticationv1.ImpersonateUIDHeader)\n\thasUID := len(requestedUID) > 0\n\tif hasUID {\n\t\timpersonationRequests = append(impersonationRequests, v1.ObjectReference{\n\t\t\tKind:       \"UID\",\n\t\t\tName:       requestedUID,\n\t\t\tAPIVersion: authenticationv1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tif (hasGroups || hasUserExtra || hasUID) && !hasUser {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"requested %v without impersonating a user\", impersonationRequests)\n\t}\n\n\treturn impersonationRequests, nil\n}\n", "numLines": 275, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 275}}

    19→import (
    20→	"fmt"
    21→	"net/http"
    22→
    23→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters"
    24→	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"

    26→
    27→// WithRequestInfo attaches a RequestInfo to the context.
    28→func WithRequestInfo(handler http.Handler, resolver request.RequestInfoResolver) http.Handler {
    29→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    30→		ctx := req.Context()
    31→		info, err := resolver.NewRequestInfo(req)
    32→		if err != nil {
    33→			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, fmt.Errorf("failed to create RequestInfo: %v", err))
    34→			return
    35→		}
    36→
    37→		req = req.WithContext(request.WithRequestInfo(ctx, info))
    38→
    39→		handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    40→	})
    41→}
    42→

    71→func addDefaultMetadata(obj runtime.Object) {
    72→	metadata, err := meta.Accessor(obj)

    74→		// if this happens, then some static code is broken

    77→
    78→	labels := metadata.GetLabels()

    80→		labels = map[string]string{}
    81→	}
    82→	for k, v := range Label {
    83→		labels[k] = v
    84→	}
    85→	metadata.SetLabels(labels)
    86→
    87→	annotations := metadata.GetAnnotations()
    88→	if annotations == nil {
    89→		annotations = map[string]string{}
    90→	}
    91→	for k, v := range Annotation {
    92→		annotations[k] = v
    93→	}
    94→	metadata.SetAnnotations(annotations)
    95→}

    20→	"errors"

    27→	authorizationclient "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/authorization/v1"
    28→)
    29→
    30→// DelegatingAuthorizerConfig is the minimal configuration needed to create an authorizer
    31→// built to delegate authorization to a kube API server
    32→type DelegatingAuthorizerConfig struct {
    33→	SubjectAccessReviewClient authorizationclient.AuthorizationV1Interface
    34→
    35→	// Compiler is the CEL compiler to use for evaluating policies. If nil, a default compiler will be used.
    36→	Compiler authorizationcel.Compiler
    37→
    38→	// AllowCacheTTL is the length of time that a successful authorization response will be cached
    39→	AllowCacheTTL time.Duration
    40→
    41→	// DenyCacheTTL is the length of time that an unsuccessful authorization response will be cached.
    42→	// You generally want more responsive, "deny, try again" flows.
    43→	DenyCacheTTL time.Duration
    44→
    45→	// WebhookRetryBackoff specifies the backoff parameters for the authorization webhook retry logic.
    46→	// This allows us to configure the sleep time at each iteration and the maximum number of retries allowed
    47→	// before we fail the webhook call in order to limit the fan out that ensues when the system is degraded.
    48→	WebhookRetryBackoff *wait.Backoff
    49→}
    50→
    51→func (c DelegatingAuthorizerConfig) New() (authorizer.Authorizer, error) {
    52→	if c.WebhookRetryBackoff == nil {
    53→		return nil, errors.New("retry backoff parameters for delegating authorization webhook has not been specified")
    54→	}
    55→	compiler := c.Compiler
    56→	if compiler == nil {
    57→		compiler = authorizationcel.NewDefaultCompiler()
    58→	}
    59→
    60→	return webhook.NewFromInterface(
    61→		c.SubjectAccessReviewClient,
    62→		c.AllowCacheTTL,
    63→		c.DenyCacheTTL,
    64→		*c.WebhookRetryBackoff,
    65→		authorizer.DecisionNoOpinion,
    66→		NewDelegatingAuthorizerMetrics(),
    67→		compiler,
    68→	)
    69→}

If nil, a default compiler will be used.\n\tCompiler authorizationcel.Compiler\n\n\t// AllowCacheTTL is the length of time that a successful authorization response will be cached\n\tAllowCacheTTL time.Duration\n\n\t// DenyCacheTTL is the length of time that an unsuccessful authorization response will be cached.\n\t// You generally want more responsive, \"deny, try again\" flows.\n\tDenyCacheTTL time.Duration\n\n\t// WebhookRetryBackoff specifies the backoff parameters for the authorization webhook retry logic.\n\t// This allows us to configure the sleep time at each iteration and the maximum number of retries allowed\n\t// before we fail the webhook call in order to limit the fan out that ensues when the system is degraded.\n\tWebhookRetryBackoff *wait.Backoff\n}\n\nfunc (c DelegatingAuthorizerConfig) New() (authorizer.Authorizer, error) {\n\tif c.WebhookRetryBackoff == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"retry backoff parameters for delegating authorization webhook has not been specified\")\n\t}\n\tcompiler := c.Compiler\n\tif compiler == nil {\n\t\tcompiler = authorizationcel.NewDefaultCompiler()\n\t}\n\n\treturn webhook.NewFromInterface(\n\t\tc.SubjectAccessReviewClient,\n\t\tc.AllowCacheTTL,\n\t\tc.DenyCacheTTL,\n\t\t*c.WebhookRetryBackoff,\n\t\tauthorizer.DecisionNoOpinion,\n\t\tNewDelegatingAuthorizerMetrics(),\n\t\tcompiler,\n\t)\n}\n", "numLines": 70, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 70}}
